 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","title":"rano v2: alerting, export, UX improvements","description":"Goals:
- Add proactive alerting capabilities for security monitoring
- Enable data export for integration with external tools
- Improve UX with presets, ancestry display, and accessibility
- Reduce friction through config validation and session naming

Context:
- Current rano is observation-only; users must watch output constantly
- SQLite data is locked inside; no easy export to CSV/Pandas/grep
- Config debugging requires trial-and-error runs
- Session IDs are opaque (PID-timestamp) not human-readable

Scope:
- 5 prioritized features with comprehensive tests and documentation
- Each feature is independent but builds on existing infrastructure
- Preserve all existing CLI behavior; improvements are additive

Quality bar:
- Every feature must include unit tests and at least one e2e test script
- E2E scripts must use the existing harness (scripts/e2e/lib.sh)
- Tests must run offline without elevated privileges
- Documentation updates for README and --help text

Acceptance:
- Each child feature has tasks with unit tests + e2e scripts + docs
- All new flags documented in README
- Config validation catches common errors before runtime","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:06.726854553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T06:36:10.708582229Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T06:36:10.708519481Z","close_reason":"All child features complete: Export (bd-1vz), Alerts (bd-2iu), Config validation (bd-n2d), Presets (bd-26d), Process ancestry (bd-1ju), Session diff (bd-1o5), Colorblind themes (bd-1lq), Session naming (bd-q9u), Retry detection (bd-1i6), Shell prompt integration (bd-36b)."}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3t7","title":"Implement preset loading","description":"Deliverables:
- PresetLoader struct:
  - builtin_presets: HashMap<&'static str, &'static str> (name -> content)
  - user_preset_dirs: Vec<PathBuf>

- load_preset(name) -> Result<HashMap<String, String>>:
  1. Check builtin_presets first
  2. Search user_preset_dirs for <name>.conf
  3. Parse as key=value, return HashMap

- Integration with config loading:
  1. Parse --preset flags early
  2. For each preset (in order):
     - Load preset config
     - Merge into base config
  3. Apply remaining CLI flags (override)

- list_presets() -> Vec<PresetInfo>:
  - Scan builtin + user dirs
  - Extract description from first line comment (# Description: ...)
  - Format for --list-presets output

- Built-in preset contents (as static strings):
  - PRESET_AUDIT, PRESET_QUIET, PRESET_LIVE, PRESET_VERBOSE","design":"Built-in preset definitions:

# audit.conf - Security auditing workflow
# Description: Full logging for security audits
json=true
log_dir=/tmp/rano-audit
no_dns=false
stats_interval_ms=0
show_ancestry=true

# quiet.conf - Minimal output
# Description: Summary only, no live output
summary_only=true
stats_interval_ms=0
no_banner=true

# live.conf - Real-time monitoring focus
# Description: Frequent stats, no SQLite overhead
stats_interval_ms=1000
stats_width=80
no_sqlite=true
stats_view=domain

# verbose.conf - Maximum detail
# Description: All information, all protocols
show_ancestry=true
include_udp=true
include_listening=true
stats_interval_ms=2000

Search order:
1. ~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf
2. /etc/rano/presets/<name>.conf (system-wide)
3. Built-in (compiled in)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.958498465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:08:43.687648105Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:08:43.687607859Z","close_reason":"Implemented PresetLoader with 4 builtin presets (audit, quiet, live, verbose), load_preset(), list_presets(), apply_preset_values(), and CLI flags --preset/--list-presets. Presets merge in order (later wins). All 78 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3t7","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:49.987421243Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3t7","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-db8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:02.835734189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-52z","title":"Implement config validation logic","description":"Deliverables:
- ConfigValidator struct:
  - errors: Vec<ConfigError>
  - warnings: Vec<ConfigWarning>

- validate_config_file(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as key=value
  - Check each key against known_keys set
  - Validate value types per key
  - Compile patterns to catch regex errors

- validate_toml_config(path) -> ValidationResult:
  - Parse as TOML
  - Check [providers] section structure
  - Validate mode is 'merge' or 'replace'
  - Check pattern arrays are strings

- validate_paths(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - log_dir: directory exists or can be created
  - sqlite: parent directory exists
  - config files: exist if explicitly specified

- validate_patterns(config) -> ValidationResult:
  - Compile all pattern/exclude_pattern as regex/glob
  - Report syntax errors with pattern text

- ValidationResult:
  - is_valid: bool (no errors)
  - errors: Vec<(file, line, message)>
  - warnings: Vec<(file, line, message)>","design":"Known config keys (for typo detection):
# Process selection
pattern, exclude_pattern, pid, no_descendants

# Polling
interval_ms, once

# Output
json, summary_only, color, log_file, log_dir, log_format, no_color, no_banner, theme

# Domain resolution
no_dns, domain_mode, pcap, include_udp, no_udp, include_listening

# SQLite
sqlite, no_sqlite, db_batch_size, db_flush_ms, db_queue_max

# Stats
stats_interval_ms, stats_width, stats_top, stats_view, stats_cycle_ms

# Alerts (new)
alert_domain, alert_max_connections, alert_max_per_provider, alert_duration_ms, alert_unknown_domain, alert_bell, alert_cooldown_ms

# Presets (new)
preset

Value type validation:
- _ms, _size, _max, _top, _width: positive integer
- pattern, exclude_pattern: compilable regex/glob
- color, log_format, domain_mode, theme: enum values
- Boolean keys: true/false","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.748149071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:23:51.994618802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:23:51.99457524Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-52z","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-n2d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:21.762516839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-90z","title":"Retry detection CLI and tests","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flags: --retry-threshold <N>, --retry-window-ms <N>
- Config keys: retry_threshold, retry_window_ms
- Warning output format: '⚠ Retry pattern: N connections to IP:port in Xs'
- SQLite: add retry_count column to events (for close events)

- Unit tests:
  - test_retry_detection_triggers_at_threshold()
  - test_retry_detection_window_expiry()
  - test_retry_detection_per_endpoint()

- E2E test: simulate rapid reconnects, verify warnings","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.710962523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:17:15.761860755Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:17:15.761801533Z","close_reason":"Implemented retry detection CLI and tests:
- Added CLI flags: --retry-threshold, --retry-window-ms
- Added config keys: retry_threshold, retry_window_ms
- Added retry_count column to SQLite events table
- Integrated RetryTracker into main loop with warning output
- Warning format: '⚠ Retry pattern: N connections to IP:port in Xs'
- Added 3 unit tests: threshold trigger, window expiry, per-endpoint tracking
All 113 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-90z","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1i6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.753292539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-90z","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3dy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:49.214275153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-94z","title":"Ancestry tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_read_ancestry_single_level() - process with no parent
  - test_read_ancestry_multi_level() - 3+ level chain
  - test_ancestry_cache_hit() - cached value returned
  - test_ancestry_cache_staleness() - recompute on comm mismatch
  - test_format_ancestry_truncation() - >5 levels truncated

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/process-ancestry.sh):
  - Create nested process tree: bash → sleep (to simulate claude)
  - Run rano --show-ancestry --pid <sleep_pid>
  - Verify ancestry displayed in output
  - Verify ancestry stored in SQLite
  - Verify report shows parent breakdown
  - Log: process tree, expected ancestry, actual output

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Process Ancestry'
  - Use case: debugging agent swarms
  - Example output with ancestry
  - Performance note: caching behavior","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:51.718741595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:12.226700916Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:12.226658195Z","close_reason":"Add ancestry tests and documentation: 21 unit tests for ancestry formatting/caching, E2E test script process-ancestry.sh, and comprehensive README section","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-94z","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:51.785639198Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-94z","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-15l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:18.654742192Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-fb6","title":"Implement shell status command","description":"Deliverables:
- StatusArgs struct:
  - one_line: bool
  - format: Option<String>
  - sqlite: PathBuf

- Template expansion:
  - {active}: active connection count
  - {total}: total connections
  - {anthropic}, {openai}, {google}: per-provider counts
  - {session_name}: current session name

- run_status(args: &StatusArgs) -> Result:
  - Query latest session from SQLite
  - Expand template or use default format
  - Output single line

- Performance: target <50ms execution
- Caching: optional, skip for simplicity initially

- Unit tests for template expansion
- Benchmark test for execution time","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:22.02124494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T06:32:47.909506667Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T06:32:47.909462093Z","close_reason":"All deliverables complete: StatusArgs struct, template expansion, run_status(), 7 unit tests passing, E2E test created and passing (8ms avg execution), README documentation added.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-fb6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-36b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:22.041849373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","title":"Alert output formatting and logging","description":"Deliverables:
- Alert output to stderr with distinct formatting:
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | domain-watch | api.unknown.com matched '*unknown*'
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:01Z | max-connections | 101 connections (threshold: 100)
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:02Z | long-duration | connection to api.anthropic.com:443 lasted 45000ms

- Colors:
  - Alert prefix: bright red background
  - Alert type: yellow
  - Details: white

- Bell: if --alert-bell, output \a after alert line

- JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with 'alert': true field

- SQLite: add 'alert' boolean column to events table
  - Alerts logged with alert=true for later querying

- Summary: include alert count in final summary
  'Alerts triggered: 5 (3 domain-watch, 2 max-connections)'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.170178182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010307656Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:25:35.010262661Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.200230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-20u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.488350819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-lr2","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-291","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.788894643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-n2d","title":"Config validation command","description":"Purpose:
- Catch configuration errors before runtime
- Reduce debugging time for 'why isn't my config working'
- Provide clear feedback on config file issues

User impact:
- 'rano config check' validates all config files and reports issues
- 'rano config show' displays resolved configuration
- Clear error messages point to exact file:line of problems

Features:
- rano config check: validate config.conf, rano.toml, presets
- rano config show: display resolved config as key=value
- rano config show --json: display as JSON for automation
- rano config paths: show where config files are loaded from

Validation checks:
- Syntax: valid key=value, valid TOML
- Known keys: warn on unknown keys (typo detection)
- Value types: numbers parse, booleans valid
- Patterns: regex/glob patterns compile
- Paths: referenced files exist (log_dir, sqlite)
- Provider config: patterns are valid, mode is merge/replace

Exit codes:
- 0: valid configuration
- 1: errors found (won't run)
- 2: warnings only (will run but may have issues)

Testing:
- Unit tests for each validation check
- E2E script with invalid configs to verify error messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.5648513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.30557649Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:27:38.305506198Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-n2d","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.59425301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-q9u","title":"Automatic session naming","description":"Purpose:
- Generate human-readable session names instead of opaque run_ids
- Enable 'remember that session last Tuesday' conversations

User impact:
- Sessions named like 'morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' instead of '12345-1705512345000'
- 'rano report --session morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' for lookup
- Easier to discuss sessions in team contexts

Features:
- Auto-generate name on session start: {time_of_day}-{primary_provider}-{pattern}-{date}
- --session-name <name> to override auto-generated name
- Store in sessions table alongside run_id
- Report and export accept session name as filter

Time of day mapping:
- 00:00-06:00: night
- 06:00-12:00: morning
- 12:00-18:00: afternoon
- 18:00-24:00: evening

Testing:
- Unit tests for name generation
- E2E script verifying names appear in report
- Test custom --session-name override","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.697380819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:31:23.908029005Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T03:31:23.907986235Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-q9u","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727782292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

  - test_csv_header_generation() - correct field order

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/export-formats.sh):
  - Seed SQLite with test data
  - Export to CSV, validate header and row count
  - Export to JSONL, validate each line is valid JSON
  - Test --since/--until filters

  - Example: 'rano export --format jsonl | jq -r .domain | sort | uniq -c'
  - Field reference table","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.683237477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050454256Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:58:11.050411055Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-uj6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.694725221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-uj6","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:58.073466211Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","title":"Implement ancestry chain reading","description":"Deliverables:
- read_ancestry(pid) -> Vec<(u32, String)>:
  - Walk /proc/{pid}/stat to get PPID
  - Recursively read parent until PPID=0 or PPID=1
  - Return chain from root to current: [(init_pid, init_comm), ..., (pid, comm)]

- AncestryCache struct:
  - cache: HashMap<u32, (Vec<(u32, String)>, Instant)>
  - ttl: Duration (default 30s)
  - get_or_compute(pid) method with staleness check

- Staleness detection:
  - If cached entry exists, verify current comm matches cached comm
  - If mismatch (PID recycled), invalidate and recompute

- format_ancestry(chain) -> String:
  - Output: 'zsh(123) → tmux(456) → claude(789)'
  - Truncate if >5 levels: '... → parent(456) → claude(789)'
  - Handle single-level: 'claude(789)'

- Integration:
  - Add ancestry_cache to main state
  - Lookup ancestry when --show-ancestry enabled
  - Pass ancestry to event formatting","design":"Implementation approach:
- Reuse existing read_ppid() from descendant tracking
- Stack-based iteration (not recursion) to avoid stack overflow
- Stop at PPID=0 (kernel) or PPID=1 (init/systemd)
- Reverse to get root→leaf order

Performance considerations:
- Cache is essential - ancestry changes rarely
- 30s TTL is reasonable (processes don't typically reparent)
- Staleness check: comm is sufficient (PID reuse with same comm is rare)
- Memory: ~100 bytes per cached entry, max ~1000 tracked PIDs = 100KB

Edge cases:
- Process exits during walk: return partial chain with '?' for missing
- Zombie process: use comm from cmdline fallback
- Init containers: stop at container init, not host init","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.224824805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313186516Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:11:08.313142694Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","depends_on_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:02:49.294596381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"rust_agent_network_observer-vb7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented ancestry chain reading + cache in src/main.rs (read_ancestry, AncestryCache w/ TTL + PID reuse check, format_ancestry truncation). Integrated optional ancestry output into connect/close event formatting (pretty + JSON) gated by args.show_ancestry. Added show_ancestry field in MonitorArgs. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T09:10:58Z"}]}
